title: B4/B8 R166 independent candidate verifier and adversarial baseline protocol

source_artifacts.selection_rule: first candidate is incumbent; replace only on Less; retain first-seen ties
source_artifacts.independence_boundary: standard-library-only verifier; no import of the R165 executor and no Qiskit call
adversarial_baselines: alter a candidate field without changing the row hash; hash must reject
alter a stored selection and recompute the row hash; independent selection must reject
drop the source-selected candidate; source-return mismatch must be detected
reverse a multi-candidate stream and record whether source selection changes
source_artifacts.claim_boundary: reproduce committed candidate-level evidence and verifier rejection behavior only; do not claim production mapping change, alternate search path, bug, advantage, BQP, solved frontier, or credit

requirements.label: the R166 protocol, contract, executor, and every R165 source artifact are hash-bound
three committed R165 profile manifests and 256 replay rows are independently loaded
row and manifest payload hashes are independently recomputed
all four policy selections are recomputed from retained binary64 leaves
source-return and policy-change aggregates match R165
the verifier does not import the R165 executor or call Qiskit
rehashed false stored selections are rejected by recomputation
removing the source-selected candidate is detected as a source-return mismatch
no production mapping, bug, advantage, BQP, solved-frontier, or credit claim is emitted
